[2018-03-09 01:37] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): can i ask for everyone's help hijacking ssp on unit and game design?  when he proposes a unit and ignores feedback, we should respond with our own version (1.1) and then only discuss our versions
[2018-03-09 01:38] f300xen (f0x): i propose that everyone mute him so we can have him not exist
[2018-03-09 01:39] jamesa7171: that seems unnecessarily mean and counterproductive; I recommend just ignoring him (as most are)
[2018-03-09 01:40] 1000mh (Henry): Mods you ruin everything >:(
[2018-03-09 01:40] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): but is it counterproductive?  we end up discussing a unit that might actually belong in the game instead of repeating "stop making up new mechanics" ad naseum
[2018-03-09 01:41] Deleted User (Deleted User): Feel free to mute or ignore him
[2018-03-09 01:41] jamesa7171: spotting a way to make one of his units good/interesting and discussing that is different than deliberately planning to do that beforehand
[2018-03-09 01:41] http404error: I think we should do things that make the #unit_and_game_design channel noncancerous, like propose good unit designs and argue about design principles
[2018-03-09 01:42] http404error: Actually good discussion can arise from bad designs, but most of the time they're not worth paying any mind
[2018-03-09 01:42] http404error: I would discourage direct responses to an individual with the aim of silencing them though
[2018-03-09 01:42] kynarethnobaka (Kynareth No Baka): and whenever possible, use tact.
[2018-03-09 01:42] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): exactly, mute/ignore leaves design channel as a wasteland where ssp tries to one up himself
[2018-03-09 01:42] http404error: Tact is a lot to ask of this playerbase.
[2018-03-09 01:42] http404error: But it's a good request
[2018-03-09 01:42] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): tact goes nowhere with ssp
[2018-03-09 01:43] kynarethnobaka (Kynareth No Baka): a person may be just really eager to offer suggestions and ideas - explain kindly what needs to change to make their ideas better fit within the rules of the game engine and philosophies of the devs
[2018-03-09 01:43] kynarethnobaka (Kynareth No Baka): being offensive only damages the community.
[2018-03-09 01:44] http404error: The tact isn't for SSP
[2018-03-09 01:44] kynarethnobaka (Kynareth No Baka): even if you dislike someone and feel their removal would not harm the community, having toxic comments on record in the minds of those who see the toxic comments will leave a permanent negative mark on the game's culture
[2018-03-09 01:44] http404error: The tact is for everyone else
[2018-03-09 01:44] http404error: Because we want the channel to be noncancerous
[2018-03-09 01:45] http404error: Explanations have proven to have no effect on this user
[2018-03-09 01:47] http404error: Just do something else, talk about something else if there's no merit to someone's contributions
[2018-03-09 01:47] http404error: I don't see anyone else designing units here
[2018-03-09 01:47] http404error: Well, that's because it's *hard*
[2018-03-09 01:48] kynarethnobaka (Kynareth No Baka): indeed. it's actually really difficult to think up something that isn't just a cost adjusted re-themed something else, the list of things present is so large already. someone having some wacky ideas isn't _bad_ per se, since it can get the thoughts moving forward
[2018-03-09 01:49] http404error: Yup, like I said though, SSP is not your audience when you're making discussions out of things
[2018-03-09 01:49] http404error: That's really important to forming a good community culture and teaching people what good design is
[2018-03-09 01:51] kynarethnobaka (Kynareth No Baka): as alpha players our job is to ensure we have a positive culture so that new arrivals feel welcome here and decide to stick around, growing the playerbase and thereby making the devs money, which ensures the game never gets deleted
[2018-03-09 01:52] http404error: I think there's a lot of design space left in Stamina, for instance, that could be tapped in a cool way in custom units.
[2018-03-09 01:52] http404error: Maybe I should post Unit Design Challenges
[2018-03-09 01:52] http404error: Those were a thing on the subreddit at one point, right?
[2018-03-09 01:53] http404error: The "and game design" part is important too - I like talking about SDR in the context of other games a lot
[2018-03-09 01:53] http404error: There's also the progression systems, game modes...
[2018-03-09 01:54] 1000mh (Henry): Thanks for working this out guys. You're all awesome

[2018-03-10 01:05] jamesa7171: @mqp I think you made very good points, but I can think of a couple counterpoints:

1. I don't think the current skin system really establishes a "fashion hierarchy" of consumerism; I think people judge each other primarily based on their displayed badges and their leaderboard rating than on their cosmetics, and so I don't think people who care about status really bother with skins. Most people (status-seeking or not) just go for skins they like.

2. Setting up a profitable gambling game is not _necessary_ unethical even if takes advantage of human weaknesses in estimating risk and value. Gambling itself is a fun activity to many people and as long as the expected fun experienced outweighs the expected value lost from the gambling then there is no actual exploitation going on (e.g. one can go to a casino and lose $50 yet still walk away happy if the experience of gambling for a few hours was worth >=$50 in entertainment value to them)
